prose: **Ferrocene Fukui Reactivity Analysis** — GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding, xtb program), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: input structure from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz` (21 atoms, 2 Cp rings + Fe center). Fukui indices computed via N+1 (cation, charge +1, mult 2) and N−1 (anion, charge −1, mult 2) single-point calculations relative to the neutral ground state. All three states converged; Mulliken partial-charge conservation check passed (sum of f⁺ = 1.0000, sum of f⁻ = 1.0000). **Most electrophilic site**: iron (atom 11), f⁺ = 0.2289 (the headline value reported). **Most nucleophilic site**: iron (atom 11), f⁻ = 0.2594. The iron center dominates the Fukui reactivity profile, reflecting its redox-active nature and central coordinating role. Carbon atoms (Cp rings) show modest f⁺ ≈ 0.0136 and f⁻ ≈ 0.0122; hydrogens show f⁺ ≈ 0.0635 and f⁻ ≈ 0.0619. Dual descriptor at Fe (f⁺ − f⁻ = −0.0305) indicates slight nucleophilic preference. Integrity: trustworthy = true. Visualization plot generated.
